steps.title: Identify the sampling population
steps.content:
The 200 people were selected at random from people who indicated they liked the book.

A valid statistical inference can only be generalized to the population from which the random sample was drawn.

Sampling population: people who like the book. Result: 95% disliked the movie.

steps.title: Apply the sampling principle to each choice
steps.content:
• A: Generalizes to all people who go see movies — this population was not sampled, so no valid inference can be made about them
  • B: Generalizes to all people who read books — we only sampled those who like THIS particular book, not all readers
  • C: Makes a claim about people who dislike the book — we sampled zero people from this group, so we have no data on them
  • D: Makes a claim about people who like the book — this is exactly our sampling population, and 95% (which is "most") disliked the movie
That confirms D is correct.
